book_title: Trade Like a Stock Market Wizard
download_status: OK

vision_analysis.stop_level: Below the cheat area low, approximately $98-100
vision_analysis.pivot_level: High of the cheat consolidation area around $108
vision_analysis.volume_characteristics: Volume dry-up during the cheat consolidation (Aug-Sep), massive volume surge on breakout attempt
vision_analysis.key_lesson: Cheat area is earliest point to trade cup pattern. Valid cheat shows volume contraction + price tightness. Stock should be up 25-100% in prior 3-36 months.
vision_analysis.trend_template_at_entry: Price above rising 200-day MA (visible), in Stage 2 uptrend
vision_analysis.kimi_notes: Classic Minervini cheat entry. The absence of a handle makes this an earlier entry point with slightly higher risk but much earlier positioning. Volume confirms institutional interest on the initial move.

book_title: Think and Trade Like a Champion
download_status: OK

vision_analysis.volume_characteristics: Volume on breakout, then contraction during squat
vision_analysis.key_lesson: Same squat behavior on cup completion cheat. Tennis ball action = bounces back like a tennis ball after being dropped. Stay with trade if no stop violation.
vision_analysis.kimi_notes: Tennis Ball Action is a key concept - the stock should bounce back quickly after a shakeout/squat. If it doesnt bounce, its a real failure not a squat.

vision_analysis.entry_level: BUY POINT at pivot breakout above ~$22-25
vision_analysis.stop_level: Below the pivot low or 200-day MA
vision_analysis.volume_characteristics: Volume expansion on breakout from base
vision_analysis.key_lesson: True market leaders bottom well ahead of the general market. AMZN gave a year to prepare for the buy point. RS vs Nasdaq Composite was turning up before the market.
vision_analysis.trend_template_at_entry: Price above rising 200-day MA, RS line turning up before market
vision_analysis.kimi_notes: Beautiful example of relative strength leadership. Bottom panel shows AMZN vs Nasdaq Composite - AMZN bottomed first and broke out while Nasdaq was still struggling. The BUY POINT annotation is exactly at the pivot breakout above the base.

vision_analysis.entry_level: Any pullback to 40-week MA during Stage 2
vision_analysis.volume_characteristics: Big up bars with abnormally large volume on rallies, lower volume on pullbacks - classic accumulation
vision_analysis.key_lesson: Stage 2 advance shows stair-step pattern of higher highs and higher lows. Accumulation signs: big volume on rallies, low volume on pullbacks. Stock may double or triple and still keep going.
vision_analysis.trend_template_at_entry: Price above rising 200-day MA, 200-day MA in uptrend
vision_analysis.kimi_notes: This is a teaching example of what Stage 2 looks like. The dashed trendlines show the stair-step nature. Volume pattern is classic accumulation. Key insight: by the time Stage 2 is underway, stock has often already doubled.

book_title: Trade Like a Stock Market Wizard
download_status: OK

vision_analysis.entry_level: Short entry on Stage 4 breakdown (Minervini Short Alert 7-9-12)
annotations: Minervini Private Access Initiates Short Alert
vision_analysis.key_lesson: Stage 3 tops form after extended moves. Broker upgrades on broken stocks are traps. CMG was put on short alert 8 days before Citigroup upgrade. Stock fell 40% in 3 months.
vision_analysis.trend_template_at_entry: Stage 4 breakdown - below 200-day MA, declining 50-day MA
vision_analysis.kimi_notes: Brilliant example of stage analysis in action. The broker upgrade was the ultimate contrarian signal - exactly when NOT to buy. Minervinis short alert timing was perfect. Note the volume spike on the breakdown.

vision_analysis.volume_characteristics: Volume distribution over extended Stage 3 top
vision_analysis.key_lesson: WTW topped in May 2011 but took a year to build a Stage 3 top before transitioning to Stage 4. Some Stage 3 tops form slowly, giving time to exit. Others (like F5) form quickly.
vision_analysis.outcome: Significant Stage 4 decline after extended top
vision_analysis.kimi_notes: Contrast with F5 on same page: WTW gave a full YEAR to recognize the Stage 3 top and exit. Different stocks form tops at different speeds. The key is recognizing Stage 3 characteristics: flattening price, distribution volume, loss of momentum.

vision_analysis.entry_level: N/A - this is a SELL example
vision_analysis.key_lesson: After 9x move in 16 months, stock doubled in 30 days. 51% in 14 days on 3 gaps. 10 of 14 days up. Classic late-stage climax.
vision_analysis.kimi_notes: Tesla 2014 is the modern version of AMZN 1999. Same signals: multiple gaps, largest up day, widest spread, high percentage of up days. Stock was extended 9x - this was obvious profit-taking territory.

vision_analysis.entry_level: Pullback to 50-day MA during market correction (~$1.50-1.75)
vision_analysis.stop_level: Below the correction low or 50-day MA
vision_analysis.volume_characteristics: Volume dry-up during sideways consolidation in bear market
vision_analysis.key_lesson: During 1990 bear market, AMGN barely dropped below 50-day MA. As market declined, AMGN moved sideways and marked time, improving RS ranking. This is how a stock improves its RS during a correction.
vision_analysis.outcome: Led the market higher when bull market resumed
vision_analysis.trend_template_at_entry: Price holding above 50-day MA while market is below 200-day MA
vision_analysis.kimi_notes: Classic RS leadership example. Top panel AMGN holding firm, bottom panel Nasdaq collapsing. The circles show AMGN pullbacks to 50-day MA - buying opportunities in a strong stock during weak market. Minervinis note I'm Buying Here confirms this was an actual trade.

vision_analysis.entry_level: N/A - this is a SELL example
vision_analysis.stop_level: N/A - sell when 40-week MA breaks on huge volume
vision_analysis.volume_characteristics: Huge volume surge on breakdown through 40-week MA - institutions jumping ship
annotations: Huge Volume Indicates Institutions are Jumping Ship
vision_analysis.key_lesson: When a stock breaks its 40-week MA on enormous volume, institutions are exiting. This is a major sell signal. Dont wait for earnings to deteriorate.
vision_analysis.kimi_notes: NFLX 2011 is the classic stage breakdown. The volume on the break was the tell - institutions were running for exits. Minervinis rule: respect the trend change. The 40-week MA break on huge volume is non-negotiable.

vision_analysis.entry_level: N/A - this is a SELL example
vision_analysis.volume_characteristics: Massive volume expansion during final 6 days
vision_analysis.key_lesson: Climax run: stock runs up 97% in 6 days. All classic sell signals present: largest up day, widest spread, multiple gaps, consecutive up days.
vision_analysis.outcome: Fell 95% from above $100 to $5.51, took 10 years to recover
vision_analysis.kimi_notes: Perfect example of climax top. Every sell signal was present: parabolic move, largest up day, widest spread, gaps, consecutive up days. This is what selling into strength looks like.

vision_analysis.entry_level: Initial buy around $11-12 (March 2004), Follow-Up Buy higher, 3rd Buy even higher
vision_analysis.pivot_level: Each new consolidation breakout becomes next pivot
vision_analysis.volume_characteristics: Volume dry-up during consolidations, expansion on breakouts
vision_analysis.key_lesson: Pyramid into winners. Initial position at breakout, add on follow-through. Each add should be smaller than the previous. Trail stop with rising MA.
vision_analysis.trend_template_at_entry: Price above rising 50/200-day MAs, Stage 2 uptrend
vision_analysis.kimi_notes: Rare insight into Minervinis actual execution. He buys initial position at breakout, then adds on follow-up buys as stock continues higher. This is pyramiding done right - adding to winners, not losers. The 3rd Buy is smaller than initial, preserving risk discipline.

vision_analysis.entry_level: After shakeout, on price surge with overwhelming volume
vision_analysis.volume_characteristics: Overwhelming volume on shakeout day = institutional accumulation signal
vision_analysis.key_lesson: Shakeout followed by overwhelming volume surge is the tip-off that stock is under institutional accumulation.
vision_analysis.trend_template_at_entry: Above rising MAs, emerging from base
vision_analysis.kimi_notes: The overwhelming volume annotation is critical - its not just any volume surge, its MASSIVE volume that signals institutions are accumulating. This is a high-conviction entry signal.

vision_analysis.entry_level: N/A - no valid entry, stock was failing
vision_analysis.stop_level: N/A - sell signal on earnings day
vision_analysis.volume_characteristics: Heaviest volume since IPO on the down day - massive institutional selling
vision_analysis.key_lesson: On Nov 1, 2007, CROX reported earnings up 144% but stock closed DOWN 36% on heaviest volume since IPO. Stories and earnings do not move prices - people do. When price action contradicts fundamentals, trust the price action.
vision_analysis.trend_template_at_entry: N/A - this was a sell/avoid signal
vision_analysis.kimi_notes: One of the most important lessons in the book. CROX had great earnings but the MARKET rejected it violently. This is called an earnings rejection or climax sell. The largest volume since IPO on a down day means institutions are dumping. Never fight this signal.

vision_analysis.entry_level: After second shakeout, on volume surge above consolidation
vision_analysis.volume_characteristics: Volume dry-up during base, surge on right-side breakout
vision_analysis.key_lesson: Two shakeouts at bottom of base clear weak holders. Price runs up right side on volume surge.
vision_analysis.kimi_notes: Double shakeout is more powerful than single - clears even more weak holders. The Big Demand annotation shows where institutional buying returns.

vision_analysis.entry_level: No valid entry - stock was unbuyable
vision_analysis.volume_characteristics: High volume on IPO, then declining volume during correction
vision_analysis.key_lesson: After IPO, Facebook failed to set up properly and corrected excessively (more than 25-35%). IPO bases need 3-5 weeks minimum and should not correct more than 25-35%.
vision_analysis.trend_template_at_entry: Failed - below key MAs, Stage 4 decline
vision_analysis.kimi_notes: Critical lesson: not every IPO is tradable. FB corrected way too much after IPO. Minervini waits for proper base formation and successful emergence to new highs. Jesse Livermore quote applies: Markets are never wrongâ€”opinions often are.

vision_analysis.stop_level: Below the handle low or squat day low
vision_analysis.volume_characteristics: Volume surge on breakout attempt, then dry-up during squat
vision_analysis.key_lesson: When a stock breaks out and squats (closes off high), dont sell immediately. Wait 1-2 weeks for reversal recovery. If no stop hit, stay with trade.
vision_analysis.kimi_notes: Important psychological lesson. Many traders get shaken out on squat days. Minervinis rule: wait for recovery. The 2-day reversal is the key signal to hold.
